name: Inspired Flight GS-ONE — NDAA-Compliant Handheld GCS
description: Purpose-built NDAA-compliant Ground Control Station with 7-inch 2000-nit sunlight-readable touchscreen. Qualcomm QCS6490 processor with 8GB RAM running Android 14. Hot-swappable batteries for all-day operations. Up to 6 miles range. Runs QGroundControl, MissionPlanner, UgCS, and Inspired Ground Control. Compatible with IF800 Tomcat and IF1200A platforms. Designed for professionals in demanding field environments.
ndaa_note: Inspired Flight Technologies, San Luis Obispo CA. US drone/GCS manufacturer. NDAA compliant.

name: QGroundControl (QGC) — Open Source GCS Software
description: Cross-platform open-source Ground Control Station providing full flight control and mission planning for any MAVLink-enabled drone. Runs on Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, and iOS. Supports PX4 and ArduPilot autopilots. Drag-and-drop mission planning, vehicle setup/configuration, real-time telemetry. Deployed in Vantis (North Dakota statewide BVLOS network) and numerous military/commercial programs.
ndaa_note: Open-source protocol (Linux Foundation). NDAA compliant — open source, no foreign entity control.

name: Mission Planner — ArduPilot Full-Featured GCS
description: Full-featured desktop GCS for ArduPilot-based vehicles. Windows primary with experimental Linux/macOS support. Advanced features include detailed vehicle configuration, log analysis, simulation, 3D visualization, DEM import, comprehensive parameter editor, and scripting support. The most feature-rich GCS for ArduPilot ecosystem. Preferred by DIY builders and developers who need deep access to autopilot configuration.

name: MotioNew M10 — Handheld All-in-One GCS
description: Portable all-in-one handheld GCS integrating computer, radio with joysticks, and datalink in a single device. 10.1-inch 1200-nit sunlight-readable touchscreen (1920x1200). Runs Linux or Windows with QGroundControl and MissionPlanner. Eliminates need for separate laptop, radio controller, and datalink. Supports multirotor, fixed-wing, VTOL, UGV, ROV, and USV control. 10-point capacitive touchscreen.

name: UgCS — Enterprise Mission Planning GCS
description: Universal enterprise-grade GCS with 3D interface supporting APM, Pixhawk, DJI, Mikrokopter, and more. Simultaneous control of multiple drones. Features include DEM import, ADS-B transponder/receiver support, Click & Go mode, joystick mode, image geotagging, and video recording. 3D view mode with binding to physical structures and built-in simulator for mission visualization.
ndaa_note: SPH Engineering, Riga, Latvia. EU/NATO allied-nation NDAA compliant. UgCS mission planning software.

name: Winmate Rugged GCS Platform — Industrial Tablet/Console
description: Rugged outdoor computing platform for GCS applications available as tablets, dual-screen briefcase consoles, or rack-mount industrial PCs. IP-rated housings, high-brightness sunlight-readable touchscreens, reinforced frames, extended temperature operation. Supports AI acceleration modules and GPUs for real-time object tracking, thermal analysis, and intelligent mission automation. Software-agnostic: runs QGroundControl, MissionPlanner, UgCS, and custom OEM integrations. Supports RF, 4G/5G, and optional satellite links.
ndaa_note: Winmate Inc., Taipei, Taiwan. Allied-nation NDAA compliant. Rugged tablet/GCS hardware.

name: General Atomics Advanced Cockpit GCS
description: Military-grade digital ground control stations for direct, real-time control of Predator/Gray Eagle-series aircraft. Can be located on any land base, in any aircraft, or on any ship worldwide. Multi-display operator consoles with full mission planning, sensor control, weapon employment, and data exploitation capabilities. Represents the high end of military GCS capability for Group 5 UAS operations.
ndaa_note: General Atomics Aeronautical Systems, San Diego CA. US defense prime. NDAA compliant. Maker of Predator/Reaper.

name: MAVProxy — Command Line GCS for Developers
description: Lightweight, extensible command-line GCS written in Python for Linux, primarily used by code developers. Provides graphical modules for map and mission editing alongside CLI interface. Extensible via Python modules for custom automation and scripting. Low resource footprint makes it ideal for companion computer integration on drones running ArduPilot.
ndaa_note: ArduPilot open-source GCS. Linux Foundation project. NDAA compliant — open source, no foreign adversary control.

name: ARK Electronics Just a Jetson — NDAA-Compliant Carrier Board
description: NDAA-compliant carrier board for NVIDIA Jetson modules providing secure onboard AI compute for defense drone applications. Designed to bring Jetson Orin capabilities into Blue UAS and NDAA-compliant airframes. Supports PX4 and ArduPilot integration. Highlighted as a 2025 breakthrough in autonomous drone compute for defense.
ndaa_note: ARK Electronics, USA. Designed and manufactured USA. NDAA compliant.

name: UAV Navigation VECTOR — Autopilot + Flight Control System
description: Professional autopilot and flight control system with integrated Iridium SATCOM for BVLOS operations. Validated integration with ATMOSPHERE SATCOM terminal for reliable, low-latency satellite connectivity. Supports fixed-wing, VTOL, and multirotor platforms. Part of the growing European autonomous drone ecosystem. Strengthens BVLOS autonomy through satellite-based C2 link.
ndaa_note: UAV Navigation, Madrid, Spain. EU/NATO allied-nation NDAA compliant. Part of Grupo Oesía defense.

name: Veronte Autopilots — High-Reliability Avionics GCS Ecosystem
description: Series of high-reliability avionics systems for real-time adaptive control of unmanned vehicles (UAV, USV, UGV). Provides both autopilot hardware and ground control software ecosystem. Features redundant architecture, DO-254/DO-178C certification path, and support for complex mission profiles. Used in military and civilian applications across Europe and globally.
ndaa_note: Embention, Valencia, Spain. EU/NATO allied-nation NDAA compliant. Veronte avionics.

name: Auterion Mission Control — Enterprise Drone Fleet GCS
description: Enterprise-grade ground control and fleet management platform built on PX4 open-source autopilot. Cloud-connected for fleet-wide mission management, analytics, and over-the-air updates. Supports US Army SRR (Short Range Reconnaissance) program. NDAA-compliant. Provides standardized interface across multiple drone platforms for defense and commercial operators.
ndaa_note: Auterion Inc., San Francisco CA (Swiss-founded, US HQ). NDAA compliant. Enterprise PX4 platform.

name: VOTIX DroneOS — Autonomous Drone Operating System
description: Autonomous drone operating system providing DFR (Drone as First Responder) and AAM (Advanced Air Mobility) capabilities. Integrates uAvionix FlightLine ADS-B data for real-time Detect and Avoid situational awareness. Enables autonomous launch, mission execution, and landing without continuous human control. Designed for public safety, security, and infrastructure inspection deployments.
ndaa_note: VOTIX, USA. US drone OS/fleet management company. NDAA compliant.
